The School of Failing to Catch the Moose credential counter
workshop_pitch: You selected Mastermind plus Performance Genius and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is lair control. Their primary moral texture is algorithmic contempt. Their exposure lateral is Reputational Self-Defense. Their core competency stack is Catastrophe Leverage. Their sidekick is a security contractor who stays because they are cultishly grateful. Their formal training comes from The School of Failing to Catch the Moose.
opening_image: The villain is less interested in victory than in teaching the room a worse definition of normal.
dossier_summary: This build runs on state detachment inside a sealed command floor. At the story level, it builds a whole environment around one plan. The primary moral texture is algorithmic contempt. The internal lie is simple: only scale can fix this. The exposure profile is reputational self-defense, which operationalized into catastrophe leverage. The local ecosystem includes a security contractor who handles hostage coordination. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission, but the full civilian rot profile also includes putting someone on speaker without warning them, leaving you on read for days and then asking for a quick turnaround, abandoning the shopping cart instead of returning it.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of algorithmic contempt shaped by reputational self-defense and confident enough to treat crisis opportunism like a normal leadership behavior. The security contractor nearby makes the whole thing feel alarmingly maintainable.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission. Pattern review now includes putting someone on speaker without warning them, leaving you on read for days and then asking for a quick turnaround, abandoning the shopping cart instead of returning it. Their current strengths remain crisis opportunism, scarcity theater, aid capture. The security contractor remains responsible for hostage coordination and is currently cultishly grateful. Do not mention the Ministry of Plots That Never Work matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the sealed command floor where lair control briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: uses charisma and spectacle to cover mediocrity or fraud. The security contractor quietly handles hostage coordination in the background, which is why the whole thing almost works. Somewhere in the room, somebody realizes too late that the sidekick is panic-prone under light pressure and will betray the villain for a sandwich.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one mastermind build will learn that the villain loses because the architecture of spectacle is broken in public. Exposure: Reputational Self-Defense. Core competencies: crisis opportunism, scarcity theater. Sidekick: security contractor on hostage coordination. Somebody is definitely auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission in act one, but the real warning is that the rot profile also includes putting someone on speaker without warning them, leaving you on read for days and then asking for a quick turnaround, abandoning the shopping cart instead of returning it.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission. The wider rot profile survives too: putting someone on speaker without warning them, leaving you on read for days and then asking for a quick turnaround, abandoning the shopping cart instead of returning it. So does the sidekick ecosystem: another security contractor arrives, still cultishly grateful, still handling hostage coordination.
sample_lines: If auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission bothers you, wait until you hear the rest of my rot profile. Then meet my security contractor
My core competencies include crisis opportunism, and my security contractor handles hostage coordination.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: This build wins by making resistance feel badly formatted.

Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be auto-rejecting a job application minutes after submission, and the wider rot profile includes putting someone on speaker without warning them, leaving you on read for days and then asking for a quick turnaround, abandoning the shopping cart instead of returning it.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn even the security contractor is hanging around because they are cultishly grateful.
